title: To The Western Wind.
content:
Sweet western wind, whose luck it is,
    Made rival with the air,
    To give Perenna's lip a kiss,
    And fan her wanton hair.

    Bring me but one, I'll promise thee,
    Instead of common showers,
    Thy wings shall be embalm'd by me,
    And all beset with flowers.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Robert Herrick, titled "To The Western Wind.", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
